data.title: Shenzhen Expressway Posts Unaudited March Toll Revenues Across Key Road Portfolio
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shenzhen Expressway reported unaudited toll revenue figures for March 2026 across its portfolio of expressway projects, led by key assets such as the Outer Ring Project, Coastal Project and Jihe corridors in the Shenzhen region, as well as significant contributions from Qinglian and GS Expressways in other parts of Guangdong. The disclosure, which details revenues by project and average daily intake, is intended as interim statistical information, with the board cautioning investors that figures may differ from future audited data due to estimation methods and settlement adjustments, underscoring that the numbers should be used carefully for investment decisions.

The company also highlighted that some toll revenue data are affected by inter-network toll collection and subsequent reconciliations, meaning monthly forecasts may be revised as actual settlement data become available. This approach reflects both operational complexity in China’s toll road network and the company’s practice of updating forecasts, signaling that short-term revenue swings at the project level may not fully capture underlying long-term traffic and cash flow trends for stakeholders monitoring its performance.

More about Shenzhen Expressway Co

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a toll road operator in the People’s Republic of China, focusing on the development, operation and management of expressways and related infrastructure. The company’s core assets are concentrated in Guangdong province, particularly in the Shenzhen region, with additional projects in other parts of Guangdong and several other Chinese provinces.

Average Trading Volume: 2,309,803

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$24.1B

data.description:

Wuhan Dazhong Dental Medical Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2651) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Wuhan Dazhong Dental Medical Co., Ltd. has announced a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.668 per share for the year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval on 29 May 2026. The dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 30 June 2026, with the exact HKD amount and exchange rate to be announced, and shareholders must be on the register by the 5 June 2026 record date.

The company also updated details on withholding tax arrangements for its H share investors, confirming a 10% enterprise income tax for non-resident enterprise shareholders and a general 10% personal income tax rate for non-resident individual shareholders, subject to applicable tax treaties and regulations. These clarifications provide greater transparency for overseas investors on their after-tax dividend entitlements and may influence investor expectations regarding net yields on the company’s shares.

More about Wuhan Dazhong Dental Medical Co., Ltd. Class H

Wuhan Dazhong Dental Medical Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company in the dental medical services sector, with its H shares traded under stock code 02651. The group focuses on providing dental-related medical services to patients in Mainland China, positioning itself within the broader healthcare and medical services industry in the region.

data.title: Qeeka Home switches auditor from PwC to BDO after fee dispute
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. ( (HK:1739) ) has shared an announcement.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. has replaced PricewaterhouseCoopers as its independent auditor after failing to reach an agreement on sharply higher audit fees linked to the group’s fast-growing international service operations. The board and audit committee determined that PwC’s proposed 2025 fee of RMB4 million, up from RMB2.8 million in 2024, exceeded the company’s budget, and invited tenders from other firms before selecting BDO with a proposal of RMB2.48 million.

The company emphasized that PwC’s resignation stemmed solely from the fee dispute rather than any issues with audit quality or governance, framing it as a mutually recognized impasse rather than a forced termination. BDO has since been appointed auditor and will conduct the 2025 audit under International Standards on Auditing using its global audit methodology, a move that may lower costs while maintaining compliance amid Qeeka’s growing international complexity.

More about Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed company that operates through a group structure and is incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability. The group has been rapidly expanding its International Service Business, which has increased its operational scale, geographical reach and the complexity of its accounting and financial reporting requirements.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Shandong Xinhua Pharmaceutical Company Limited Class H ( (HK:0719) ).

Shandong Xinhua Pharmaceutical Company Limited reported essentially flat operating income of RMB 2.42 billion for the first quarter of 2026, while net profit attributable to shareholders fell 25.2% year on year to RMB 83.9 million and earnings per share dropped to RMB 0.12. The profit decline reflects higher operating and R&D costs and weaker operating cash flow, with net cash from operating activities swinging to an outflow of RMB 116.6 million, even as total assets were broadly stable and net assets attributable to shareholders edged up 2.7%, signaling margin pressure despite a largely unchanged revenue base.

The company’s weighted average return on net assets decreased to 1.59% from 2.21%, underscoring reduced profitability and efficiency in capital use. Management has emphasized that the figures are unaudited but prepared under Chinese accounting standards and cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care when trading the company’s securities in light of the softer earnings and cash flow profile.

The company focuses on producing and selling pharmaceutical products and related healthcare offerings in both domestic and international markets.

data.title: Shenzhen Expressway Lifts Q1 Profit on Strong Core Earnings Despite Flat Revenue
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) has issued an update.

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited, a PRC-based toll road and expressway operator listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai, earns most of its income from operating, investing in and managing highway infrastructure. The group’s business is embedded in China’s transport infrastructure sector, where regulated tariffs and traffic volumes drive earnings and cash flows and shape its long-term investment capacity.

In the first quarter of 2026, the company posted revenue of RMB 1.76 billion, virtually flat year-on-year, while total profit rose 8.3% and net profit attributable to shareholders increased 6.5%. Excluding non-recurring items, core net profit grew 10.7% and operating cash flow climbed 4.8%, indicating stronger underlying profitability despite modest top-line movement.

Basic and diluted earnings per share edged down to RMB 0.192 from RMB 0.203 and weighted average return on equity slipped to 2.10%, reflecting capital base expansion that outpaced profit growth. Total assets were broadly stable at about RMB 71.0 billion, while equity attributable to shareholders rose 1.6%, suggesting a slightly stronger balance sheet even as short-term returns on equity moderated.

The unaudited figures signal resilient operations and improving earnings quality for Shenzhen Expressway, which may reassure investors about cash generation in a capital-intensive sector. However, the combination of flat revenue and lower ROE underscores the ongoing challenge of extracting higher returns from a growing asset base in a heavily regulated toll road market.

More about Shenzhen Expressway Co

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a Chinese joint stock company that operates, invests in and manages highways and related infrastructure projects. Listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai, the group focuses on toll road and expressway operations, generating revenue primarily from traffic flows across its portfolio of transport assets in the People’s Republic of China.

The company’s activities position it within the broader transport infrastructure and toll road sector, where cash flow stability, capital intensity and regulatory oversight are key factors for shareholders and creditors. Its performance is closely tied to regional economic activity, traffic volumes and policy changes affecting road tariffs and concessions.

As an infrastructure operator, Shenzhen Expressway balances operational efficiency with long-term asset management, and its quarterly disclosures provide investors with updates on revenue trends, profitability and balance sheet strength. These metrics help stakeholders assess the sustainability of dividends, funding capacity for new projects and the company’s competitive standing in China’s expressway market.

data.title: Xiaomi Sets 2026 AGM to Approve 2025 Results and Share Capital Mandates
data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Xiaomi ( (HK:1810) ).

Xiaomi has called its annual general meeting for 2 June 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements for 2025 and receive reports from the board and external auditor. The meeting will also vote on the re-election of executive and independent non-executive directors, authorize the board to set director pay, and confirm the reappointment and remuneration of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor.

Investors will consider mandates allowing the board to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, excluding treasury Class B shares, within a defined mandate period. Shareholders will also vote on granting directors broad authority to allot, issue, sell, or transfer Class B shares and related convertible securities or options, which could provide Xiaomi with flexibility for future capital management and equity-based transactions while potentially affecting share capital structure and existing holders’ stakes.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1810) stock is a Buy with a HK$41.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Xiaomi stock, see the HK:1810 Stock Forecast page.

More about Xiaomi

Xiaomi Corporation is a China-based technology company that designs and sells smartphones, consumer electronics, and smart home devices, and also operates an internet services ecosystem. The company is listed in Hong Kong with dual counters in HKD and RMB and targets both domestic and global mass-market consumers through competitively priced, connected products.

data.description:

An announcement from Guangdong Kanghua Healthcare Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3689) ) is now available.

Guangdong Kanghua Healthcare Group has announced a change to the book closure period for its register of members related to the 2025 annual general meeting. The new closure dates run from 15 June 2026 to 18 June 2026, during which no share transfers will be processed, and shareholders on the register as of 18 June 2026 will be eligible to attend and vote.

The adjustment shortens and shifts the original book closure window while keeping all other details of the previously issued annual results, circular and AGM notice unchanged. This update clarifies procedural requirements for shareholders, including the deadline for lodging share transfer documents, helping ensure orderly determination of voting rights at the forthcoming AGM.

More about Guangdong Kanghua Healthcare Co., Ltd. Class H

Guangdong Kanghua Healthcare Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated healthcare group listed in Hong Kong that operates medical and healthcare facilities. The company focuses on providing clinical and related health services to patients in mainland China, positioning itself within the broader private healthcare services industry serving the Chinese market.
